name: noa-relay
description: NOA approval Relay — a hosted, framework-free (node:http) transport that routes gate-signed holds + encrypted-display ciphertext to the approver's device and carries the phone-signed decision back. The relay NEVER signs and NEVER holds a private key: a compromised relay can at worst cause denial-of-service or spam, never a forged approval (Red Line 3 / invariant 2). It is NOT the gate — grants, consumption, uncertainty and timeout-receipt SIGNING all live at the local gate (spec §8), never here.
